item.title: That’s the perfect example of nothing needs to be said it just needs to be done
item.dc:creator: tanijrou

item.title: Time for another unhinged XL Friday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
forbidden water is the most tantalizing
gonna be frog city around here soon
wouldn't be my first pick
i so wish that i had kept all my slammers. had a lot of really cool ones
ok but coke gives you the anti munchies
yes, you might've thought something else was coming when scrolling
can't tell you how many times a friend has busted out a random animal lung and asked me to identify
mmm hmm
we've all done this
here it is.. favorite of the bunch
wish i was that cool
right..
(it's a Big Lebowski reference)
looks nice but i don't know how to knit. i sort of know how to read at least
gorillas are so cool
good times
that's all for me.. have a great weekend gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Curry Bday
item.description:
Happy 78th Birthday!
The first movie I heard your sultry voice...
Loved you from then on
I was so giddy when I saw your name in other movies.
I quote many lines from Clue.
Sexy evil voice
Sorry for the repeat
Best laugh ever!
item.dc:creator: 42QuestQueen4

item.title: Her spooky vaginosis cleared up

Just a quick heads up to fellow Caroline fans who may not have heard about her big announcement yesterday--she's "committing to an 18-year bit": https://youtu.be/nXEkxMT6RgQ
(And an excuse to dump some of my favorite Caroline vids).

item.title: Eat Your Heart Out! 100 New Vintage Images of People and Food!
item.description:
Here's your goddamned bread, 1960s
1977 red-headed fabulous lady, looks like Lobster Thermidor. Eat up, honey!
A week’s worth of groceries for For $12.50, 1947
Cake # 1 - talk about layer cake!
Ladies with Tupperware popsicle makers that I remember using as a kid, 1977
Steak chart from Merry Man Tavern in Akron, OH. My choice is rare to medium rare - please note that well done isn't an option.
1986 school lunch - you need TWO drinks?
Dinner at Casa Susana, a transvestite vacation home, NY, 1950s
Pear and Gooseberry pudding, 1973
Did you know Pepperidge Farms made Star Wars cookies? 1983
Love this outdoor double folding chair meat display, impressive decoration on the main slab, 1974
White Castle needs you to look yourself over, 1950s
Dave, make sure you touch the whole plate of meat, thanks, 1955
Camping breakfast while looking at the back of the cereal boxes, 1975
Waitress, 1977
Crab feast with hats and a Pastafarian. But why bleach on the table? 1960
McDonald's playground, horror tree, 1976 (photo by Bill Owens)
Cake # 2, Charlie's birthday, love the belly button
Doris eating in hospital after having her daughter. Check out the phone, the bed (wood frame in hospital!) and the sink, 1952
I could look up what a buckwheat groat is, but I prefer to remain ignorant, 1973
The pig roast, Kansas, 1982
1970s baby shower
1930s kitchen, colorized
The glorious Harvest Gold kitchen of 1975
Corn, 1900s
Cake # 3, the most crazed wedding cake of all time - eight cakes on top of a large sheet cake. The groom is not really featured. 1960s
Little lady ready for that turkey, 1940s
Black Bananas? How? Why? 1984
Giant Farmer Bob holding sausage and cheese at a Mobil Gas Station in Portage, WI, 1980s (he's still around today)
Naples, Italy, 1954 - wow, his shoes (photo by Sabine Weiss)
I call this the two-fisted gullet-stuffer.
Salvatore Scalco's wedding - he owned National Fruit Company, Wash, DC, 1921
Great spinning buffet table at Mendenhall Motel in Mendenhall, MS, 1950s
Preparing Irish Coffee in Dublin, Ireland, 1964
Pie-eating contest in Peacham, VT, 1950s - the pies are so tiny!
Blue birthday cake with bonus twins, 1958
Cake # 4 - No More Yellow
Jan nibbles away on the veggie platter, 1962
Big buffet, but why the indoor umbrella? 1950s
Singer Jaye P. Morgan (remember Match Game in the 70s? Anyone?) has lunch with friend. Love the sauerkraut and dill pickles on the table, NYC, 1954
What are they eating? Shrimp? Earl Carroll restaurant, Hollywood, CA, 1948
1970s United Airlines meal
Candied apples, 1945
The Coffee Mold, 1967
McDonald's, France, 1980. I see no Royale with Cheese here.
Atkins Diet, Day 1
There's food but that living room is akin to being punched in the face with the 1960s
1960s family BBQ while camping
No, I don't know why no burgers on Tuesday.
Jerry and his chest hair prep the green beans. Hope he throws out the ones in his ears, 1950
The Fish
Adapted kitchen/dining table for little people, Germany, 1928
1958, nice Thanksgiving spread, but look at the incredible salt and pepper shakers collection!
Holy Shit!
Starting with grapefruit, nice wallpaper, 1963
Cleaning the lingonberries, Sweden, 1970
Woman getting baked beans from Horn & Hardart Automat, NYC, 1955 (photo by Arthur Rothstein)
Food Show in Wash DC, Borden Creamery booth featuring Moon Girl Butter, 1922.
Cake # 6, Peanuts and Mickey Mouse
The butter, egg and cheese truck, NYC, 1943 - and Hitler's Children! (photo by John Vachon)
1973 prom or wedding
Having a smoke, a Coke and cooking in a cave, 1971
Watermelon kids, 1973
Junior BBQ master, 1972
Chuckie, Provincetown, MA, 1980 (photo by Joel Meyerowitz)
I kinda miss the milk cartons in elementary school - they would arrive early morning in a crate outside the door to the classroom and just sit there til lunchtime. I wanted that chocolate milk immediately.
Too much bologna, 1970s - the child also seems stunned.
The dilemma of a cake but no plates, 1978
1970 fish is ready to cook!
Thanksgiving, 1952
The waitress, 1950s
Mom and daughter make kebabs, 1962
Actress Simone Simon enjoys a succulent Chinese meal, 1930s
Great 70s fashion and Automat for snacks, Netherlands, 1971
Three Jell-O mold dishes at the Christmas party! Milwaukee, WI, 1951
The souffle - nice Amish facial hair, 1970s
Chili and Ritz Crackers! 1975
My kind of picnic - bread, wine, cheese, 1972
Stop lying about it being from scratch, Ruth!
Cake # 7 - World's Largest Birthday Cake, Seattle World's Fair, 1962
Officer Big Mac getting some love, McDonald's, 1970s
Just a cold corned beef lump, 1981
1976 family fondue dinner, Des Moines, IA
Cake # 8 - Jonathan's bar mitvah with bonus uninvited photo bombers, 1972
Oyster guy found actual string of pearls! 1955
Lobster feast, 1955
Kingaburger in Malibu, CA, 1964
Eye-catching ad campaign at the National Potato Chip Convention in Chicago
Banana Burt and his gal Lil at their Dairy Queen, love the floating bananas, Buzzards Bay, MA, 1950
A sausage tree on display during International Green Week exhibition, Berlin, Germany, 1976
Couple gets the flambé treatment at the Three Keys restaurant at Winthrop Hotel, Tacoma, WA, 1970.
Super Giant Supermarket in Rockville, MD, 1964 (photo by John Dominis)
Wrigley Company and it's World's Largest Store Counter (one million sticks of gum!) New York World's Fair, 1939
Supermarket Institute Convention with Peter Pan and his peanut butter, 1973
I have no context here, just the heebie-jeebies
Candy making at Nut Tree Candy in Vacaville, CA, 1960s
Martha Raye's NBC TV show with the wonderful dancing Kellogg's cereal boxes, NYC, 1952
Early 80s dinner, sister is wearing a turtleneck, a polo shirt and a sweater. The son looks like the mean brother from the Get Out movie.
At the soda counter, drinking a ice cream soda and SPINNING A PLATE on a stick! This kid is amazing, 1959
Marie Merrington's cooking album, Gay Gourmet, 1950s
item.dc:creator: FAUST44

item.title: Ninja Turtles and the Wu-Tang Clan - 90's New York Hip Hop Diorama
item.dc:creator: ChalupBatmanSugarBottomTheThird

item.title: Very nice picture of the M33 galaxy
item.description: The Triangulum galaxy, also known as Messier 33 (M33), is theoretically the farthest object visible with the unaided eye. But, in practice, you’ll need perfect dark sky conditions – and good eyesight – to see it with the eye alone. Even with binoculars and a telescope, this pinwheel-shaped spiral galaxy is a challenge to spot. It’s relatively nearby. But it’s turned face-on to us, so it has a low surface brightness in our sky. The Triangulum galaxy, named for its location in the constellation Triangulum, is the 2nd-nearest spiral galaxy to our Milky Way, after the Andromeda galaxy (M31). If you do manage to glimpse it, you’re looking across a distance of 2.7 million light-years. ______________________________ Picture from Cathrin Machin Space Art @Cathrinmachin on Twitter
item.dc:creator: cl1978

item.title: It sucks that it's this hard to get people to understand, but I did it!
item.description: I have a neurological condition that prevents my brain from producing enough dopamine and serotonin to allow for focus on complex tasks. Music has been shown to cause these chemicals to be produced, and as such it is easier for me to work with an earbud in for me. If course, this sounds a lot better than "I have ADHD and the music makes my brain feel good so I can work harder." Because some people don't believe in ADHD because it's so often over diagnosed.
item.dc:creator: NotaScientologist

item.title: This is some American shit I'm down for
item.dc:creator: ImpatientMoth

item.title: When you remind people that the bible says to take care of the poor, needy, and homeless...
item.description: I deal with this a lot. Customers coming in and complaining about the homeless shelter. I'm giving up on citing data on how their tax money, annually, is less than a good pizza. Now I'm just quoting bible scriptures. That usually stops them.
item.dc:creator: ctsawood0

item.title: A moderately sized DK dump of stolen memes for these trying times 1399.
item.description:
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.

.

.
Fun stuff.
item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: Truth
item.description: Got a new assistant manager at my job that isn’t the best at her job but she’s a body to fill the space. Especially cause she’s been working overtime at other stores. She is constantly complaining about working so much. Currently she has a messed up wrist(sprained or broken) and she refuses to get a real diagnosis because she might miss work, it’s all because she’s a big spender. She is really strict on her kids but from what she says she rarely spends time with them. To be honest I didn’t think she had kids because of the amount of time she works. I’d much rather be with my kids than working!
item.dc:creator: banoot

item.title: I don't know why I love this so much, but I do... She helped a kid who needed it when others might not have bothered
item.description: Edit : I just saw the tags... I DID NOT tag this as wholesome... I think it's lovely, I think it's great that the kid went to the right person... But I wouldn't necessarily say it's wholesome.... It's just something I thought was worth sharing
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: They make billions off our backs then get exposed for being cheats, and this is the guy who goes to jail? Man, fuck this job!!
item.dc:creator: kanchuka

item.title: Surely there's at least 500 UK citizens on Imgur?
item.description:
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/659071 Please sign it if you are a UK citizen. If not, please upvote this post to have it on FP when they wake up! Anti karma whore comment: https://imgur.com/gallery/DRH1mIX/comment/2390981609 ~~ So, just over two weeks ago, I made a post explaining how a campaign called "Stop Killing Games" was trying to, well, save some games from a preventable death: https://imgur.com/gallery/i019Dna The TL;DR is that some video games are designed to require constant connection to a server hosted by the publisher/developer. And when they decide to shut down said server, you end up with a "dead" product that you potentially paid good money for. This campaign is pushing against that. And if we get 500 UK citizens to sign the above petition, this issue should be looked at by the UK Government. If we're lucky they might even do something!
The latest example of "dead" games is The Crew, a racing game by Ubisoft that was shutdown on the 1st of April (great choice there Ubi, nice joke). If anyone tries to start the game, all you'll get is an error screen telling you "Connection to proxy failed". The game is totally and utterly unplayable. Even physical copies are not working.
And Ubisoft didn't even stop there. They went out of their way to remove the licence from EVERYONE (include that one gif here). So not only can you not play the game, you can't even download it anymore. Oh, what's that? You wanted to try and reverse engineer it? "Well better have a backed up copy sucker!"
And now I can hear some of you saying: "Yeah but that game was released in 2014, you had 10 years to play it. Whatever, what's the deal?" Well the deal is that, at release, it cost upwards of 85 bucks with the option to purchase in game credits. And since it had around 12 million players, there's most likely a few people who spent (at least) a couple hundreds on the game. Hundreds of monies that were paid for a product no one can access anymore. And its not like purchasing groceries, goods that are perishable. Customers paid for a product that could potentially last forever (unless WW3 starts soon and all computers are gone ¯\_(ツ)_/¯ ), but was rendered unusable by the seller. A seller telling you to "[...] check the store to pursue your adventures[.]" ... I'm in awe at the disrespect
And if you were thinking "Meh. I don't care about The Crew or Ubisoft, I'm not concerned", you better think again. More and more games are released as "on-line only", so at some point it will most likely concern you. Your kids are going to be impacted, that's for sure. No retro gaming for them if games keep going this route. "So what, game companies should keep their servers running for ever?" No, that stupid. We all realise that. But they should be obligated to have an End of Life plan for their games, to keep them playable no matter what. "But this is going to require work from the companies!" Yup. And I don't care. I'm a simple man: I buy a product that should work for pretty much ever, I want it to. "Ok, so how do you get companies to do that?" We get legislation passed! Or we make sure that current laws are not being violated by, let's say, EULAs telling you "you only have a licence to use the game". This might actually be illegal, and EULAs can't be above the law. And if we get a win *anywhere* this should have a ripple effect worldwide! Plus, this is not only about The Crew, we are targeting all "on-line only" games, using that one as a "vessel", so to say.
So if you are a UK citizen, you can help! Sign the petition: https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/659071 You could also ask family members and friends to sign it... Just saying! If you're not, please upvote this post so that I reaches FP when they wake up ;) Anti karma whore comment: https://imgur.com/gallery/DRH1mIX/comment/2390981609 If you have additional questions on the campaign, a FAQ is available here: https://www.stopkillinggames.com/faq Thank you a lot for reading my ramblings and sorry for any spelling or grammar errors. PS: Dang it forgot about the licences. Yeah The Crew was probably killed because of that (car or music licences). But Spec Ops: The Line had the same issue and was simply *delisted*, not rendered unplayable!
item.dc:creator: AsocialOddDuck

ARTIST: GLEN BROGAN
ICM SUGGESTED TRACK: [ ▶ & SCROLL] Johnny Cash - Folsom Prison Blues YouTube: https://youtu.be/AeZRYhLDLeU?si=a8PHONwxOoSneLTa Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/track/0LTSNmOLBt25GMjHlxp9OR?si=41fe389693794ab5

ICM SQUAD: @vazquezdaniel, @ohvalleyofplenty, @nichoDriver, @Dirgekins, @onewallpaperaweek, @thedownlowe, @TheForbiddenSemicolon, @bagoffishstix, @JayKillBam, @majkongskull, @DingoJ0nes, @HandsomePenguin, @MrJwinkyface, @Gorgoror, @ciadmillefailte, @kaeldra ICM TIP JAR: https://www.patreon.com/InterwebChexMix Just to let you know, we do not take credit for any random art, comics, or photos featured in this collection; we only take credit for the daily effort of compiling the collections themselves. Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos
item.dc:creator: MetaPathos
